chief_complaint: An 82-year-old man presented with persistent complex visual hallucinations involving past episodic scenes.
history: The patient had a history of post-stroke epilepsy (PSE) and presented with complex visual hallucinations (CVH) that resolved with levetiracetam and were maintained with zonisamide. A literature review suggested that CVH may occur when epileptic activity spreads to non-dominant temporal or parietal regions.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: Electroencephalography revealed epileptic discharges in the right posterior temporal and parietal regions.
correct_diagnosis: Post-stroke epilepsy with complex visual hallucinations
differentials: Post-stroke epilepsy with complex visual hallucinations
correct_ranking: Post-stroke epilepsy with complex visual hallucinations
